title: Data Analyst
description:
About this work area
The Digital & Expansion team sets the digital direction, ensuring IKEA leads in a fast‑changing world. The team develops and delivers brilliant digital products and services, using smart technology and agile methods to bring ideas to life, quickly and thoughtfully. It is a home for digital talent, growing skills and knowledge to keep our digital capabilities strong and ready for the future. Together, we build a brighter, more accessible digital experience for IKEA and our customers.
Responsibilities

Champions data‑driven decision‑making promoting and ensuring the use of insights to significantly improve business performance.
Conducts advanced data exploration and analysis leveraging modern data mining techniques and visualisation tools to deliver actionable insights for stakeholders.
Evaluates and integrates diverse data sources, assessing both internal and external structured data for product analytics and strategic decision‑making.
Write demanding SQL queries that extract, transform and load data from storage to an appropriate format.
Translate data into compelling narratives collaborating cross‑functionally to ensure data solutions meet business needs and continuously generate value.

Qualifications
You have a strong foundation in data analysis, encompassing data mining techniques, modern visualisation software (e.g. PowerBI), and expertise in SQL and scripting languages like Python for sharing insights. Your knowledge extends to probability, statistics (including A/B testing), and an understanding of leveraging large datasets for improved business decision‑making. You thrive in cross‑disciplinary environments, balancing speed with rigor, and possess excellent communication skills for compelling data storytelling to diverse audiences. Crucially, you understand the complexity of the IKEA business and the integrated role of data & analytics within it.

Formal qualifications (BSc, MSc, PhD) in economics, engineering, natural sciences, computer science, statistics or mathematics or an equivalent field.
A minimum of 2 years of professional experience as a Business Analyst, (Junior) Data/Web Analyst, or (Junior) Data Scientist.
Good knowledge of data processing for large datasets.
Preferred familiarity with cloud computing environments, such as Amazon Web Services or Google Cloud Platform.
Preferred experience with digital product development in a retail setting.

Additional information

Permanent, full‑time, 76hrs/fortnight.
The location for the role is Service Office, Tempe NSW, with a minimum of 3 days a week on‑site.
Upon successful selection, employee will be required to obtain a national police check.

title: Data Analyst
description:
Built World Media PTY LTD
We are seeking an experienced Data Analyst to support the collection, analysis and interpretation of market, event and business intelligence data. This role will help drive strategic decision-making, improve reporting capability and enhance client outcomes across FuturePlace commercial, research and event portfolios. 
As a Data Analyst, you will work with data from multiple sources, including event platforms, CRM systems, market research databases and business intelligence tools. 
You will be responsible for analysing complex datasets, identifying trends and preparing clear reports, dashboards and insights for internal stakeholders and external clients. 
This role is suited to someone who is analytical, commercially minded and confident turning data into practical business intelligence. 
In this role, you will: 

Collect, validate and analyse large and complex datasets from multiple sources, including event platforms, CRM systems, market research databases and business intelligence tools. 
Design and maintain data models, dashboards and reporting frameworks to support business planning and performance measurement. 
Identify trends, patterns and insights relating to industry sectors, customer behaviour, event performance and commercial activities. 
Conduct statistical analysis and data interpretation to support strategic business initiatives and client projects. 
Develop and automate regular reporting processes to improve operational efficiency and data accuracy. 
Collaborate with internal stakeholders to define business requirements and deliver data-driven solutions. 
Prepare reports, visualisations and presentations for management and external clients. Monitor data quality and implement procedures to ensure data integrity and compliance. 
Support market intelligence and research projects through quantitative analysis and forecasting. Recommend improvements to data collection methodologies, reporting systems and analytical processes. 

To be considered for this role, you will need: 

A relevant qualification in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Economics or a related field. 
At least one to two years of relevant experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar analytical role. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex datasets. 
Experience using data analysis and visualisation tools. 
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and SQL. Experience preparing reports, dashboards and business insights for senior stakeholders. 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects simultaneously. 
Competitive salary package plus superannuation. 
Opportunity to work with leading organisations across the property, infrastructure and technology sectors. Exposure to high-profile industry events, research and advisory projects. 
Professional development and career growth opportunities. 
Collaborative and innovative work environment. 

To apply, please submit your CV and cover letter outlining your experience and suitability for the role.

description:
Looking for an opportunity to add immediate value to a master data cleanse project?
We are currently seeking an experienced Data Quality and Remediation Analyst to support our big-brand client in the Consumer Health and FMCG space! 
Use your expertise in data and reporting to rectify accumulated legacy errors, duplicate records and data discrepancies across product, vendor, customer and pricing/promotional registries.
This role will allow you to truly take initiative in a fast-paced, corporate scale-up environment where you can truly make an impact!
What's in it for you?

 Work for a leading brand in the Consumer Health and FMCG sector! 
Up to $700 p/d + super (depending on candidate experience)
Work as part of a small, collaborative team environment across finance and merchandise functions 
Monday - Friday, 40 hours per week 
Based from the Sydney CBD office - hybrid flexibility encouraged! 
Opportunity to work fully autonomously, whilst still having support and resources available to you 
Temporary position for 3 months, with the possibility of extension 
Flexibility with start and finish times, promoting a true work/life balance 
Integrate into a sociable group on professionals that are incredibly passionate about what they do

 The Role
  Drive Data Quality Excellence 
 - Conduct detailed data audits across multiple platforms, including ERP, supplier, and e-commerce systems, identifying and resolving discrepancies, duplicates, formatting issues, and inaccurate records.
 Lead Large-Scale Data Cleansing Initiatives

Analyse recurring data and system issues, pinpoint underlying causes, and implement practical controls and validation checks to improve data accuracy and minimise future errors.

 About You
 You thrive in a fast-paced, evolving environment, posses and obsessive attention to detail and can hit the ground running immediately!

Be committed to finishing the task, leaving no stone unturned! 

If this sounds like a project that you can sink your teeth into, please  apply now!

title: Data Analyst
description:
The role
As a Data Analyst, you will support the delivery of analytics initiatives that underpin organisation‑wide strategic priorities. Working as part of a high‑performing data and analytics team, you will contribute to the tactical execution of projects across the team’s portfolio and help build and maintain data products that enable informed decision‑making.
This role is ideal for someone who has developed strong foundations through a graduate program or early‑career analytics role leading strategic initiatives in a hands‑on, stakeholder‑facing environment. You will partner closely with teams across the business to translate data into meaningful insights and contribute to scalable, value‑driven analytics solutions.
About you:
You are curious, proactive and data obsessed, someone who enjoys asking questions, taking initiative, and owning solutions from insight to impact. You’re a strong team player who thrives on collaboration and building trusted partnerships across the business.
You can tell a clear, compelling story with data, translating complex technical analysis into commercially focused insights that drive strategic decision‑making. Comfortable working in ambiguity, you balance curiosity with accountability and aren’t afraid to challenge assumptions to deliver real value.
You bring energy and positivity to your work and enjoy being part of a growing analytics team that the business actively seeks out as a partner.
What you’ll do

Collaborate proactively with non‑technical stakeholders to support end‑to‑end analytics initiatives, delivering insights and flagging risks or dependencies early
Support cross‑functional collaboration by working closely with data, technology, and business teams
Demonstrate financial acumen when assessing the impact of existing initiatives and evaluating new opportunities
Translate business requirements into practical data analytics solutions that support strategic priorities
Contribute to the prioritisation, delivery, and continuous improvement of analytics projects within the team’s portfolio
Apply foundational and emerging advanced analytics concepts to solve business problems and support decision‑making
Develop data products, reports, and visualisations that enable stakeholders to interpret insights quickly and confidently
Build and deliver clear presentations and communications that explain analytical findings, assumptions, and implications to non‑technical audiences
Assist with data transformation, cleansing, and modelling to ensure analytics outputs are accurate, reliable, and well‑governed

What you need – required qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Analytics, Business Analytics, Data Science, Actuarial Studies, or a related technical discipline
Minimum of 3 years’ experience in a data, analytics, or business intelligence role
Proficiency in SQL and at least one programming language used in data analytics or data science (e.g. Python, R, Scala, Spark)
Experience using at least one business intelligence or analytics platform (e.g. Power BI, Tableau)
Experiencedeliveringlarge scaleend-to-endanalytics in cross-divisionaltransformationprojects, with the ability to prioritize and manage scope.
Solid understanding of data transformation, data cleansing, relational databases, and data modelling concepts
Understanding of data governance principles, including documented definitions, calculations, data sources, and lineage
Experience communicating insights and analytical outputs to non‑technical stakeholders
Ability to apply analytical thinking and structured problem‑solving to real‑world business challenges

Preferred qualifications

Exposure to Microsoft Fabric and/or Databricks
Experience working with ERP and/or CRM systems
Proven ability to help track and articulate benefits delivered from data‑driven initiatives
Experience supporting analytics initiatives within Operations, Sales, and/or Marketing environments
Exposure to advanced statistical reasoning, exploratory data analysis, or hypothesis‑driven analysis
Experience contributing to analytics best practices, frameworks, or reusable data products
Experience working in a complex or enterprise data environment
Experience developing solutions using the Microsoft Power Platform (e.g. Power Automate)

title: Associate Quantitative Analyst (Economic Data)
description:
Contribute to the transformation of Economic Group's quantitative data capabilities
Apply quantitative and statistical techniques to real-world economic datasets
Join a collaborative team that sits at the intersection of economic data, technology, and business transformation, supporting forecasting, modelling and policy analysis

About the roleThis is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or final-year university student with background in Actuarial Studies, Economics, Statistics, Data Science or other quantitative disciplines to apply their skills to real-world economic data.
As part of the Economic Analytical and Technical Services (ECATS) team in the Reserve Bank's Economic Analysis Department, you will support the development and enhancement of the Department's quantitative data environment used for economic modelling, forecasting and policy analysis.
This role is best suited to candidates who enjoy working with time-series data to support economic insights, modelling and analysis. It is an opportunity to continue developing your technical and analytics skills in a collaborative team environment.
Your responsibilities
Maintain and enhance economic datasets used in forecasting and quantitative analysis
Support development and optimisation of data pipelines for modelling and analytical use
Perform data validation, statistical checks and quality assurance to ensure integrity of analytical outputs
Assist in preparing and administering data inputs for econometric models, forecasting frameworks and policy analysis
Collaborate with economists, analysts, and technical specialists to improve data usability for modelling and research
Respond to data queries with a focus on analytical accuracy and interpretation

About you
Recently completed or in final year of a degree in Actuarial Studies, Economics, Statistics, Data Science or other quantitative disciplines
Proficiency in R, Python or SQL, with strong data manipulation capability (Excel expected)
Interest in applying data to economic analysis, forecasting or policy-related modelling
Strong attention to detail, particularly in data quality, validation and reproducibility
Curious mindset with an interest in understanding what the data means-not just how to manipulate it
Demonstrated ability to solve structured analytical problems

Be MoreWorking for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.
Why RBA?
The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.
The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

description:

Lead and manage the Residential Sales Incentive Program.
Drive actionable insights from large data sets, dashboards and reports.
Hybrid working, sociable team and many career development pathways.

About Domain And CoStar GroupDomain is one of Australia's most trusted and loved property brands, helping millions of Australians find their dream home and stay informed about the property market. Now, Domain has joined forces with CoStar Group, a global leader in real estate information, analytics, and online marketplaces to take the next big leap in property technology and data innovation.
As part of CoStar Group, an S&P 500 company with more than 35 years of experience transforming how people understand and transact real estate, Domain is entering an exciting new era of growth. CoStar is investing heavily in new products, technology, and people to revolutionise how Australians access property data, insights, and marketplaces. Develop new tactical reports that enable uncovering future risks and opportunities.

Curious, self-motivated and high attention to detail.

Are you a data-driven problem solver who loves turning complex information into meaningful insights? Mediahub is looking for a Data Analyst to join our growing team and help shape the future of data and analytics across a portfolio of exciting brands.

title: Junior Business Intelligence (BI) Report Developer
description:
The Law Society of NSW is Australia's largest and most influential membership association for solicitors. We're not all lawyers, but we work together to support the legal profession and ensure a just society for all. 
We are currently looking for a permanent full time Junior Business Intelligence (BI) Report Developer to join our IT team.
About the role
Support the development and maintenance of high-quality Power BI reports and data solutions that enable business decision-making across the Law Society. The Junior BI Developer focuses on building foundational technical capability while contributing to the design, usability and visual effectiveness of reporting outputs. You will work closely with senior team members and stakeholders to translate requirements into clear, engaging and user-friendly reports. You will support continuous improvement of reporting standards while operating in line with organisational data governance and compliance requirements.
What you'll be doing
 Reporting and Data Solution Delivery - assist in the design, development and maintenance of Power BI reports and dashboards to meet business requirements. Focus on report visual design, layout and usability to ensure they are intuitive, user-friendly and aligned with organisational design and usability standards. Apply visual design principles including layout, hierarchy, colour and accessibility to enhance report usability and user experience. Data Management and Governance - apply data governance, security and privacy requirements in the development of reports. Apply data governance requirements and support compliance with organisational policies and privacy obligations and assist in report auditing and identifying data quality issues. Technical Development and Optimisation - assist in developing and maintaining SQL queries, data transformations and Power BI models. Support testing and validation. Identify simple optimisation opportunities. Stakeholder Engagement and Support - support end-user queries and escalate appropriately under the Business Intelligence team frameworks. Assist with requirement gathering and training. Documentation and Standards -develop and maintain documentation including data definitions, business rules, and user guides. Enforce BI development standards, frameworks and best practices. Ensure that requirements, testing and approvals are appropriately recorded and documented.  Project and Continuous Improvement Contribution -participate in projects as a supporting team member by taking responsibility for assigned tasks, engaging with user acceptance testing (UAT) and providing deliverables by due dates. Collaboration and Knowledge Sharing -collaborate within the IT and broader Law Society of NSW teams and promote knowledge sharing and continuous learning across the team and organisation. Support knowledge transfer within the Business Intelligence team and engaging with peer review processes.

About you
 Up to 2 years’ experience as a BI Developer (preferably in Power BI), data analysis or similar. Completion of tertiary qualification in Computer Science, Information Systems, Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering or equivalent.

Desirable:
 Beginner to intermediate experience in SQL/T-SQL Foundational knowledge of data concepts such as relational and non-relational databases, Data Warehouses and Data Lakes. Exposure to Azure and other tools in the Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate). Exposure to Canva design software. Exposure to ticketing and project management tools such as JIRA or Azure DevOps. Exposure to GIT version control management.

Skills: 
 Analytical and problem-solving capability, with keen interest in understanding the underlying data and business rules. Basic to intermediate technical capability in Power BI (Desktop & Service) and data visualisation tools. Basic technical capability in SQL Server Management Studio and similar tools. Ability to translate requirements into simple reporting outputs. Strong interest in data visualisation, design and user experience. Ability to design clear, visually engaging and user-friendly reports. Attention to layout, consistency and accessibility against templates and internal standards. Understanding of data accuracy, quality and validation principles. Awareness of data privacy, security and governance requirements. Sound judgement, professionalism and integrity, including appropriate escalation of tasks and identification of potential risk. Good communication skills and ability to engage with non-technical users. Collaborative mindset and willingness to learn from others. Ability to manage tasks and meet deadlines with guidance. Strong attention to detail. Commitment to continuous learning and development.

About the team
The Junior Business Intelligence (BI) Report Developer sits within the Information Management function of the IT department, which delivers and supports enterprise systems, data and analytics capabilities across the organisation. You will operate within the Business Intelligence team and require frequent collaboration with data integration developers, application developers, business analysts and business stakeholders. You will contribute to the organisation’s data strategy, including establishing a single source of truth and improving data-driven decision-making.
To apply
The Law Society of New South Wales is committed to building and maintaining a respectful and inclusive workplace, appointing the best person for the role and supporting diversity. 
Applications should contain a CV and cover letter that outlines your key experience and motivations for this role.

title: Demand Analyst
description:
Welcome to Haleon. We’re a purpose-driven, world-class consumer company putting everyday health in the hands of millions. In just three years since our launch, we’ve grown, evolved and are now entering an exciting new chapter – one filled with bold ambitions and enormous opportunity.
Our trusted portfolio of brands – including Sensodyne®, Panadol®, Advil®, Voltaren®, Theraflu®, Otrivin®, and Centrum® – lead in resilient and growing categories. What sets us apart is our unique blend of deep human understanding and trusted science.
Now it’s time to fully realise the full potential of our business and our people. We do this through our Win as One strategy. It puts our purpose – to deliver better everyday health with humanity – at the heart of everything we do. It unites us, inspires us, and challenges us to be better every day, driven by our agile, performance-focused culture.

Leading global brands.
An additional 5 wellness/ leave days per year
Health and Wellbeing benefits and programs
Hybrid working
Bonus
Parking Provided

Hi. We’re Haleon. A world-leading consumer healthcare company with exceptional category-leading brands – including Sensodyne, Centrum, Panadol, Otrivin. We have a clear purpose: to deliver better everyday health with humanity. For billions of people around the globe.
As a Demand Data Analyst, you will play a key role in analysing consumption trends and customer behaviour, leveraging a range of data sources and insights to support accurate demand forecasting and effective inventory management. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure insights are translated into clear, actionable outcomes that drive business performance.
This is a 9-month contract opportunity to start in August, providing parental leave cover, ideal for someone who enjoys stepping into a role, making an immediate impact, and working in a fast-paced, collaborative environment. You’ll gain exposure to a leading consumer healthcare business, partnering with experienced commercial and supply chain teams while contributing to well-known, category-leading brands.
A day in the life:

Analyse historical consumption and sales data to identify trends and patterns that impact demand forecasting.
Develop and maintain demand forecasting models using statistical techniques and data analysis tools.
Collaborate with marketing, sales, and supply chain teams to gather relevant data and insights.
Create and present reports and dashboards to communicate findings and recommendations to stakeholders.
Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) related to demand and inventory levels.
Assist in the development of forecasting promotional strategies based on demand insights.
Utilize advanced analytical tools (e.g., Advance Excel, Power BI) to manipulate and analyse large datasets

Your areas of knowledge and expertise

Proven experience in demand planning, forecasting, or analytical roles within FMCG or consumer healthcare environments is preferred
Strong analytical capability, able to quickly interpret data, identify trends, and translate insights into actionable outcomes
Hands-on experience with forecasting tools and demand planning systems
Demonstrated ability to analyse historical data, identify demand drivers, and improve forecast accuracy in a fast-paced environment
Confident stakeholder management skills, partnering cross-functionally with Sales, Marketing, Category, and Finance
Proactive and adaptable, able to manage competing priorities and deliver outcomes quickly in a dynamic contract environment

Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion.

title: Power BI Consultant
description:

With close to 100 consultants and more than 10 years delivering successful data outcomes for our customers, our teams work on interesting and diverse projects with organisations ranging from large local enterprises to SMEs and start-ups across multiple industries. This role will work closely with business stakeholders, data teams, and technology partners to deliver high-quality reporting and dashboard solutions that enable informed business decision-making within a large enterprise banking environment. This role is focused on:  Developing, enhancing, and maintaining Power BI reports and dashboards. Designing intuitive wireframes and dashboard layouts that effectively communicate business insights. title: Senior Data Analyst
company: National Centre for Vocational Education Research
description:
Senior Data Analyst About NCVER
The National Centre for Vocational Education Research (NCVER) is Australia’s leading provider of vocational education and training (VET) research and statistics.
We collect, manage, and analyse national VET data, translating it into trusted insights that inform decision-making across government, industry, training providers and the broader community.
At NCVER, your work directly contributes to strengthening the evidence base that supports skills development, employment outcomes and economic growth in Australia.
More detail of NCVER's activities can be found on our website.
About the role
We are seeking an experienced Senior Data Analyst (7+ years’ experience) to join our Product and Data division.
In this role, you will design and deliver forward-looking analytics that support evidence-based decision-making. You will work with NCVER’s rich data assets, alongside external datasets, to produce high-quality, accessible insights that are timely, relevant and trusted.
You will collaborate within a multidisciplinary team of technical specialists and VET experts to deliver analytics that support a skilled and capable Australian workforce.
Our work draws on tools such as SAS/SQL, Python and R, and includes methods such as data linkage, regression analysis and machine learning.
You will lead and contribute to end-to-end analytics projects - from interpreting stakeholder needs and designing analytical approaches through to delivery, analysis and dissemination.
A key part of the role involves translating complex data into clear insights through high-quality reports, presentations and data visualisations for both technical and non-technical audiences.
About you
The successful candidate will require the following skills and experience:

An analytical mindset with proven ability to think critically and creatively to solve problems
Solid understanding of advanced quantitative methods and the ability to apply appropriate techniques to complex questions
Strong experience working with large and complex datasets, including querying, analysing and interpreting data (Databricks, SAS, Python or SQL preferred)
Proven ability to deliver end-to-end analytics projects
Strong organisational and project management skills, with the ability to work independently
Experience mentoring or advising others in quantitative analysis
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex analysis into clear insights for diverse audiences
Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to collaborate with internal and external stakeholders
A degree in a relevant numerate discipline (e.g. Statistics, Mathematics, Social Sciences)
Experience in the VET sector (highly regarded)

A position description outlining full details of the role, including the knowledge, skills and experience we are seeking, is available on our Portal https://www.ncver.edu.au/about-ncver/careers/current-vacancies.

Ongoing role based in Adelaide (candidates in Sydney, Melbourne, or Canberra may be considered with travel to Adelaide required).
Hybrid working from office/home arrangement

Gifted leave over Christmas shut down
Full-time equivalent salary will be in the range of $105,000 - $125,000 commensurate with skills and experience (plus 12% superannuation) .

title: Data Analyst - University of Sydney Ancestry & Health Genomics Lab
description:
Full time, fixed term for two years   
Exciting opportunity to be a Data Analyst with the University of Sydney Ancestry & Health Genomics Lab (Hayes Lab)
Base Salary starting from $93,345 + 17% superannuation 

About the opportunity
The successful candidate should have an Honours or Master’s degree in Bioinformatics, Computational Science, Data Engineering or equivalent, and/or have a Bachelor’s degree with substantial evidence of experience working within HPC environments and big data analytics, familiarity with bash commands, with preferential experience with using command-line interface bioinformatic tools. 
The successful candidate will join the University of Sydney Ancestry & Health Genomics Lab (Hayes Lab) working on uncovering the drivers of prostate cancer health disparities. Based within the ‘state-of-the-art’ Charles Perkins Centre, this hybrid international team is both generating and interrogating multi-omic data sources to address clinically relevant questions related to developing a globally inclusive system for prostate cancer care. 
The day-to-day tasks will involve running and maintaining the computational pipelines, data backup and security, while showing initiative for workflow improvements and integrative models. Providing data quality control, filtering, and preparation for downstream analysis. Running and managing computational resources both locally and for the larger consortium within the High Performance Computational (HPC) infrastructure. Run standard pipelines for variant calling, while working with all members of the Hayes Lab from Research Officers, Postdocs, international Fellows, and PhD students, as well as the staff and members of the Sydney Informatics Hub (SIH). The successful candidate will be responsible for releasing and managing data within the team core, managing computational resources and user access, reporting to the lab head, ensuring the highest level of data integrity, while resolving data issues and performing data analytics as directed. 
The successful candidate should have a passion for working with large data sources, have strong skills in problem-solving and computational literacy, be results-orientated while placing emphasis on attention to detail, efficiency in planning and deliverables. The candidate should be able to work both within a team environment and independently and be willing to spend two days a week in the office engaging with colleagues directly. The candidate should have a passion for working in an academic environment, which includes research focused on clinical impact.
Your key responsibilities will be to:

data management for the lab and consortium
managing HPC computational access, KSU management and usage
submitting of genomic data for publication
assist in data collection activities and ensure compliance with set guidelines
support data extraction, transformation and preliminary visualisation activities
engage in data analysis and assist in identifying patterns to support decision making
assist with data preparation for internal surveys and support external submission requirements
participate in data cleansing and verification processes to ensure data accuracy
perform routine large-scale data processing for variant calling using established pipelines
coordinate with the team to produce quality reports, verify data accuracy within university systems and support compliance initiatives
communicate with a broad range of stakeholders
report directly to the Lab and Consortia lead.

About you

bachelor’s/honours/masters degree in a relevant field or an equivalent combination of relevant training and experience
experience analysing and providing insights on complex quantitative and qualitative data
experience working with large data sources and complex data across a large and diverse organisation
experience utilising analytics platforms and developing actionable recommendations
experience working in the higher education sector
experience in people management, including managing staff performance and development
skills in data analysis, accuracy and attention to detail, organisation and time management, research
computer literacy, familiarity with bash commands, with preferential experience with using command-line interface bioinformatic tools
ability to communicate effectively and work collaboratively in a team.

Pre-employment checks
Your employment is conditional upon the successful completion of all pre-employment or background checks required for the role in terms satisfactory to the University. Also, to meet the University’s obligations under the National Higher Education Code to Prevent and Eliminate Gender-Based Violence you will be asked to declare if you have been investigated for, or found to engaged in, sexual harm or gender-based violence in the course of previous employment or in a legal process. title: Data Scientist/Analyst - Graduate Role
description:
Company DescriptionTCCD is a privately owned, family-founded business built to be the leading independent grocery sales growth partner in Australia. We're not a corporate. We're not a multinational. We're a family-owned business with family values — where people know each other by name, leadership is accessible, and decisions are made quickly by people who care about the outcome.
We've been recognised with six Supplier of the Year awards across Metcash, Foodland, and Ritchies. We manage a $40M+ national portfolio of world-class tier one brands. Our leadership team brings over 100 years of combined FMCG experience — and in a flat structure, you don't just report to them, you learn from them.
We're hiring a graduate data scientist to join a small, high-performing team to accelerate analytics on a tier one portfolio across Australia's independent grocery channel. This is a 4-day role — deliberately designed to match the workload, not squeezed from 5. The fifth day is yours — extend your studies, invest in your own development and/or enjoy the work/life balance.

Produce monthly Promo ROI analysis — actual vs. expected, baseline impact, rate of sale
Run pre-promo analysis: expected uplift, margin impact, cannibalisation risk
Support range reviews with scan performance data (Circana/IRI)
Prepare field briefing materials and cycle priorities
Build dashboards and automated reporting — if you can see a better way to present the data, build it

What We're Looking For
Recent graduate — data science, analytics, commerce, economics, or quantitative discipline
Strong Excel skills and genuine curiosity about data — Python, Power BI, SQL, or similar is a bonus, not a requirement
Interest in how data drives commercial decisions — not just modelling in isolation
Commercially curious — you want to understand why an investment worked, not just that it did or didn’t

Self-driven — you manage your workload with discipline and minimal supervision

Clear communicator — you can translate data into a story that a buyer or a sales rep can act on

Why TCCD?


4-day week — structured to match the workload, not compressed from 5. The fifth day is yours

$80,000 package inclusive of superannuation

Tier-1 brands — world-class commercial development and credibility on your CV from day one

Flat structure — direct access to the CEO, NBM, and senior leadership. Your ideas are heard, your contributions are visible, your impact is measurable

Mentoring, not managing — leadership with 100+ years of combined FMCG experience is accessible and invested in your growth. You won't be left to figure it out alone

Real commercial ownership —genuine analytics, not just reports. You will sit in buyer meetings, present data, and defend recommendations

Data science runway — you'll work with Circana/IRI scan data, promotional ROI models, and real commercial datasets. If you want to build predictive models, automate reporting, or develop dashboards — bring it. We'll back you

PURPLE values — Partnership, Unity, Results, Positivity, Laughter, Excellence. These aren't on a poster — they're in our quarterly awards, our daily conversations, and the way we work

Walsh Bay offices — daily barista, breakfast, and end-of-trip facilities

Career progression — exposure and development through skill development and experiences

Who This Role SuitsThis role is designed for a graduate who wants more than a grad program:

A data science or analytics graduate who wants to apply their skills to real commercial problems
A commerce graduate who is curious about data and wants to build analytical skills alongside commercial experience
A postgrad student who wants real ownership and a day to continue their studies
Someone who wants to learn FMCG from the inside — promotional mechanics, trade spend, buyer negotiations, category management — with data as their lens
Someone who values autonomy, ownership, and direct access to leadership over a big-company grad program

title: Data & Analytics Graduate
description:
About the Insurance Council of Australia (ICA) 
The Insurance Council of Australia (ICA) represents approximately 90% of the private sector general insurance industry. We work with our members, consumer groups, and all levels of government to support consumers and communities when they need it most. 
Our purpose is to be the voice for a resilient Australia, advocating for positive outcomes for our members, our people, and the broader community. 
About the role 
As a Graduate, Data & Analytics, you will support ICA’s data strategy and contribute to evidence-based decision-making. 

Build dashboards, datasets, and insights 
Analyse trends and research across natural disasters, insurance, and affordability 
Turn complex data into clear, compelling stories 
Support data quality and governance 
Collaborate across teams on high-impact projects 

About you 
You are a curious and analytical early-career professional who enjoys solving problems and turning data into meaningful insights. 

Degree (or near completion) in Data Science, Economics, Statistics, Engineering or similar 
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail 
Experience with Excel, SQL, Python or Power BI 
Clear communication skills 
Curiosity, initiative, and willingness to learn 
Interest in insurance, climate or policy is a bonus 

Why join us? 

Purpose-driven work that makes a difference 
Supportive team and strong mentorship 
Real hands-on experience from day one 

Launch your data career with impact—apply now.
